setting.note: The rural north of El Salvador on the eve of the civil war, seen across a single day from a peasant farming family in the hills of Chalatenango. The map runs from the dirt roads and cornfields of a poor hamlet out to the departmental town, and up into the highland country toward the Honduran border where the National Guard, the army and the first guerrillas collide.

rights.originalWork: In copyright. Manlio Argueta, first published in Spanish as Un día en la vida, 1980. This book is mapped as a cited work: we store none of its text and reproduce no passages. The places are facts and the notes are our own.
rights.textSource: None stored. A cited book reproduces no text.
rights.territoryNotes: Locations and original commentary only. No reproduction of the copyrighted text, in any territory.
rights.attribution: One Day of Life, Manlio Argueta (1980). Locations mapped by Read the Places; not affiliated with or endorsed by the author or publisher.

palette.note: The red-brown of the Chalatenango dirt roads and worked earth the family walks all day. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper.
